title: Game Security Research
description: Client trust, authority models, and secure game design.

topics.title: Client Trust Issues
topics.explanation:
Any data on the client can be modified. Never trust:
- Position, health, inventory (authoritative on server)
- Timestamps from client
- Claimed hit results

Design: server validates all critical actions.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Client Trust Issues
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Client Trust Issues in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Economy Abuse
topics.explanation:
Cases: duplicated items, price manipulation, currency generation via logic bugs.

Prevention: server-side transactions, audit logs, rate limiting.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Economy Abuse
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Economy Abuse in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Logic Flaws
topics.explanation:
Examples: race conditions in trading, integer overflow in damage calc, state machine bypass.

Methodology: threat modeling, fuzzing game actions, code review.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Logic Flaws
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target

topics.title: Authority Models
topics.explanation:
Models:
- **Server authoritative** — gold standard for competitive
- **Client authoritative** — acceptable for co-op casual
- **Hybrid** — client prediction + server reconciliation

Source: Gabriel Gambetta's networked physics articles.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Authority Models
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment

Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Secure Game Design
topics.explanation:
Principles:
- Server validates everything critical
- Obscurity is not security
- Encrypt + authenticate packets
- Behavioral analysis and reporting
- Regular security audits
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Secure Game Design
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
